title: Boat Rental in Reykjavik: Costs, Seasons & Tips for 2026
html:
Renting a boat in Reykjavik opens up the stunning Faxaflói Bay, with departures from the Old Harbour. Prices for a half-day self-drive motorboat start from around €350, and you can compare verified boat listings for boat rental Reykjavik on BoatHire24, the worldwide marketplace.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Reykjavik?

Costs vary by boat type and duration, from €350 for a half-day self-drive to over €3,000 for a crewed motor yacht. Shared whale-watching tours cost about €80 per person. A small motorboat such as a QuickSilver 605 rents from €350 for four hours. For a larger experience, a full-day catamaran for up to 12 guests is around €1,500. Crewed yachts with skipper and crew cost from €2,500 for an eight-hour charter.

  • Shared tour: from €80 per person (whale watching or sightseeing)
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): from €350 for up to 6 guests
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): from €1,500 for up to 12 guests
  • Crewed yacht full-day: from €2,500 for up to 10 guests, including crew

Where to set off in Reykjavik

Most charters begin from the Old Harbour (Faxaflói Harbour), home to several operator offices. Nearby marinas include Geirsgata 7 dock and the small boat harbour at Ægisgarður. For day trips, head to the island of Viðey, just 2 nautical miles offshore, or explore the longer reaches of Akurey and Engey. The fishing town of Hafnarfjörður, 15 minutes south, has its own harbour with further rental options. For longer voyages, consider departing from Kópavogur or Garðabær.

Which boat to rent

BoatHire24 lists a variety of vessels suitable for Reykjavik's waters. Browse the Princess V58 listing for a luxury motor yacht experience.

  1. Motorboats (6, 8 guests): Best for fast island hopping or fishing trips; half-day from €350.
  2. Rib boats (up to 12 guests): Excellent for wildlife tours and coastal exploring; half-day from €500.
  3. Sailing yachts (6, 10 guests): A peaceful way to enjoy the fjords; half-day from €600.
  4. Luxury motor yachts (8, 12 guests): Fully crewed with amenities for a premium day out; full-day from €2,500.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Reykjavik?

For most powerboats under 15hp you do not need a licence, but many rental companies require a valid boating licence or a brief practical test. The operator will confirm the exact requirements at booking.

In summer, the midnight sun extends daylight until nearly midnight, meaning you can set off from the Old Harbour even for an evening cruise. The prevailing wind is from the east, so plan your return to avoid choppy seas.
High season runs from June to August, with July being the warmest and busiest month.

At a glance

  • Price band: €80 to €3,500
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Typical trip length: 4 to 8 hours
  • Guest capacity: 6 to 12 passengers
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Reykjavik

Can you rent a boat in Reykjavik without a licence?

Yes, but only for boats under 15hp or on guided tours; for larger vessels a licence or operator briefing is required.

How far is Viðey Island from Reykjavik harbour?

Viðey Island lies about 2 nautical miles north of the Old Harbour, a 15, 20 minute boat ride.

What is the best month for boating in Reykjavik?

July offers the mildest weather and the longest daylight hours, ideal for full-day charters extending into the midnight sun.

Are guided fishing trips available?

Yes, many operators offer guided trips targeting cod and haddock, typically on RIB boats or small motorboats, with all gear provided.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Reykjavik or list your own. If you own a boat, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach travellers seeking unique on-water experiences in Iceland's capital.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Reykjavik and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
